title: Spectrophotometric Determination of Magnesium(ll) with 2-Carboxy-2"-hydroxy-3' , 5' -dimethylazobenzene-4- sulphonic Acid
author: S. B. GHOLSE
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Laxminarayan, Institute of Technology, Nagpur-440 010 

 Chemistry Department, Nagpur University, Nagpur-440 010 

  Manuscript received 18 October 1982, revised 24 August 1983, accepted 25 March 1984  

 2-Carboxy-2 ' -hydroxy-3 ' ,5 ' -dimethylazobenzene-4-sulphonic acid (CHDMAS) is recommended as a chelating agent for the spectrophotometric determination of magnesium (II). It forms a red coloured complex with magnesium(ll) which can be quantitatively determined at  p H 11.0. The complex has absorption maximum at 510 nm and it obeys Beer's law over 0.5  \(\mu\) g to 16.0  \(\mu\) g Mg(ll) per ml. The reagent forms 1 : 1 complex with Mg(ll). The method is sensitive and fast.
